title: Flowlink Sprint 02 — Shorts Pipeline & Content Engine
goal: Design a repeatable Shorts production system that turns creator workflow insights into high-clarity short-form content, maps each asset to a destination, and establishes a lightweight publishing rhythm.

steps.title: Design the Shorts System Around Reusable Narrative Patterns

sections.body: Build Shorts from narrative patterns that can be reused: myth-busting, before/after contrast, teardown, contrarian insight, and live problem diagnosis.
sections.heading: Start with repeatable hooks

sections.body: Your content is a bridge. Every Short needs to move the viewer from a passive listener to an active participant.
sections.heading: Conversion Chains

objectives.description: Create one problem-first template centered on a repeated creator bottleneck.
Create one proof-first template that shows a workflow compression or time-saving outcome.
Test your templates by mapping them to 3 different specific creator pain points.

steps.title: Map Your Content to Destination Pages

prompts.text: Which of your 3 templates feels most natural to produce weekly?
Where does the conversion chain most likely break in your current system?

tasks.description: Write a script for the Problem-Agitate-Solve template.
Write a script for the Before/After template.

payload.content: Using the templates you defined in the challenge, write three 60-second scripts. Focus on the core transformation.

questions.question: What makes a Shorts template reusable rather than one-off?
questions.expected_answer: Repeatable narrative structure and defined audience pain.
